purpose: Surfaces whose bare consonants collapse under norm() but whose meaning differs. The distinguishing signal is a harakah on the CONTENT letter, a hamza seat, a shadda, or a same-root polyseme that must be denied outright. norm() must never decide any of these. If the distinguishing diacritic is absent in the source text, the decision is pending — never the commoner reading.

fields.surface_or_norm: the colliding surface (with diacritics) or the norm() key that two readings share
fields.rule: the operational test (which helper, which letter, what to read)
fields.wrong_gloss: the gloss that shipped or would ship if norm() decided
fields.correct: the defensible reading, or 'pending' when the diacritic is absent
fields.example_loc: a quran:S:A:W where the correct reading holds

decide_on_content_letter: The harakah may sit AFTER a و/ف proclitic. Read the FIRST radical of the content word, not the first character. haraka_on/is_man_who/shadda_on already scan past the proclitic.
fallback: If distinguish_by cannot be evaluated because the source is undiacritized, emit decision=pending (reason hamza_sensitive_homograph or multi_sense_root). Prefer pending over wrong.

quarantines.rule: is_man_who(tok): مَن 'who' has no kasra on the mīm and no shadda on the nūn; مِن 'from' has kasra on the mīm.
haraka_on(tok,'م')==KASRA → preposition مِن 'from/among'; NOT مَن 'who'.
Proclitic وَ hides the signal; read haraka_on(tok,'م')==KASRA → وَمِنَ 'and from'. is_man_who('وَمِنَ') is False. This is the exact bug that glossed it 'and whoever'.
Proclitic فَ; haraka_on(tok,'م') has no kasra → فَمَن 'so whoever' (is_man_who True). Distinguish from فَمِن 'so from'.
shadda_on(tok,'م') is False → لِمَا 'for what / to that which' (lām + mā). لَمَّا has the shadda.
shadda_on(tok,'م') is True → لَمَّا 'when' or 'not yet'. Never collapse with لِمَا 'for what'.
haraka_on(tok,'ك')==DAMMA → كُلّ 'all / every'. كَلَّا (fatḥa on kāf) is 'nay!'.
haraka_on(tok,'ك')==FATHA → كَلَّا 'nay! / by no means'. Not كُلّ/كُلًّا 'all'.
haraka_on(tok,'ن')==KASRA → نِعْمَ 'how excellent!' (verb of praise). نَعَمْ (fatḥa) is 'yes'.
haraka_on(tok,'ن')==FATHA with sukūn final → نَعَمْ 'yes'. Not the praise verb نِعْمَ.
shadda on the nūn AND final ي (not ى) → أَنِّي 'that I'. bare() preserves ي≠ى; do not fold.
shadda on the nūn AND final ى (alif maqṣūra) → أَنَّى 'how / whence / from where'. Keep ى≠ي.
haraka_on(tok,'ل')==FATHA + sukūn on mīm → لَمْ negative jussive '(did) not'. لِمَ (kasra) is 'why?'.
haraka_on(tok,'ل')==KASRA → لِمَ 'why?' (interrogative lām + mā). Not the negation لَمْ.
norm() drops the hamza seat, collapsing onto أَيْمَان. Certify with norm_strict()/QAC: root أ م ن, initial hamza-kasra, ي is a glide → 'faith'.
root ي م ن, the ي is a radical → 'oaths' (plural of يَمِين). norm_strict() keeps it apart from إِيمَان 'faith'.
hamza on the first stem letter → root أ م ر 'they command'. Not يَمُرُّونَ (shadda on ر, root م ر ر) 'they pass by'. norm() erases both signals.
shadda on the rāʾ, no hamza → root م ر ر 'they pass by'. Distinct root from يَأْمُرُونَ.
norm() yields a لين-shaped key, but this is إِلَى + ـنا (preposition + 1pl enclitic), not a triliteral stem. Parse the enclitic; never certify root ل ي ن.
Phrase-form collision with مَا لَكَ 'what is for you'. Deny that reading; allow only the م ل ك 'Owner/Master' sense when QAC certifies, else pending.
quarantines.correct: Owner / Master (root م ل ك), else pending

quarantines.rule: Same-root (م ل ك) polyseme quarantine: ٱلْمُلْك is 'the dominion / sovereignty'. Deny the مَلَك 'angel' / مَلَائِكَة sense; distinguish مَلِك 'king', مُلْك 'dominion', مَلَك 'angel' by vowels and context.
Root ن ه ر polyseme quarantine: plural نَهْر → 'rivers'. Deny the نَهَار 'daytime' sense.
Root س ل م polyseme quarantine: the maṣdar of Form IV → 'submission (to God) / Islam'. Deny the bare سَلَام 'peace' gloss; it is a distinct derivation.
Adverb/preposition عِنْدَ 'with / in the presence of / according to'. Deny the root ع ن د 'stubborn / obstinate' (عَنِيد/عِنَاد) sense — different lexeme.
Root ح ر ر maṣdar of Form II → 'freeing / manumission (of a slave)'. Deny the حَرّ 'heat' sense; this is a nominal gloss, not a verb.
Ordinal from ث ل ث → 'third'. Deny the cardinal 'three' (ثَلَاثَة) gloss — ordinal, not cardinal.
Referent guard: root ح ل م 'forbearing'. When the referent is Ibrāhīm (e.g. 11:75), gloss as the human attribute 'forbearing', NOT as a divine Name. Sense holds; referent fixes register.
Referent guard: active participle of ص ل ح → 'righteous / good (deed)'. Deny the proper-noun reading 'the Prophet Ṣāliḥ' unless context certifies it; default is the descriptive adjective.
shadda on the nūn → verb مَنَّ 'to bestow / confer a favour'. is_man_who returns False here; do not gloss as مَن 'who'.
Root م ل ك triple homograph: مَلِك (fatḥa-kasra) 'king'; مُلْك (ḍamma-sukūn) 'dominion'; مَلَك (fatḥa-fatḥa) 'angel'. Decide on the vowels + context, never on norm().
quarantines.correct: king / dominion / angel per vowels+context, else pending
